service.name: Airtable API
service.description_for_ai: Flexible database-spreadsheet hybrid. Tables, records, views, formulas, attachments. 300K+ companies use it. Use for data management, project tracking, CRM-lite, content calendars, inventory.

capabilities.description_for_ai: Get records from an Airtable table with optional filters, sorts, and field selection. Perfect for reading database entries.

base_id.description: Airtable base ID from URL: airtable.com/{BASE_ID}/... — starts with 'app'

table_name.description: Table name exactly as shown in Airtable (case-sensitive) e.g. 'Tasks', 'Contacts', 'Products'

view.description: View name to apply its filters/sorts (e.g. 'Grid view', 'Active Tasks')

fields.description: Array of field names to include: ['Name', 'Status', 'Due Date']

offset.description: Pagination offset from previous response's offset field

fields: offset (for next page if more records exist)

capabilities.description_for_ai: Create a new record in an Airtable table with field values.

fields.description: Field values: {"Name": "New task", "Status": "In progress", "Due Date": "2024-12-31", "Assignee": ["rec_userId"]} — linked records use record IDs

agent_hints.base_id: Get base ID from Airtable URL: airtable.com/{BASE_ID}/..., looks like 'appXXXXXXXXXXXXXX'
agent_hints.token: Create token at airtable.com/create/tokens. Scope: data.records:read, data.records:write
agent_hints.formula: filterByFormula supports Airtable formula syntax: {Status}='Active', AND({Price}>100, {Stock}>0)
agent_hints.pagination: Use offset token from response for next page. Max 100 records per request.
